$comment: Reference case for terminal_velocity (v_t = sqrt(2*mass*g / (airDensity*area*Cd))). Two coefficients (g, Cd) but they enter ONLY as the ratio g/Cd — they are individually non-identifiable. So this case asserts fit quality (R²/RMSE/convergence) and deliberately omits per-coefficient bounds: the fitter can recover any (g, Cd) pair on the correct ratio and still be correct.
notes: 30-point sweep (10 masses × 3 areas × 1 air density). v_t ranges ≈4–18 m/s across the sweep. No coefficient bounds because g and Cd alias through the g/Cd ratio — a regression that breaks the sqrt or the ratio structure shows up as an R²/RMSE breach instead.
